examples.title: Algorithm: Process Creation
examples.description: Demonstrates the steps involved in creating a new process
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateProcess
INPUT: parentProcess
OUTPUT: childProcess

BEGIN
 Allocate resources for childProcess
 Initialize childProcess's PCB
 Handle address space for childProcess
 Place childProcess in ready queue
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the key steps in creating a new process: allocating resources, initializing the PCB, handling address space, and placing the child process in the ready queue.

examples.title: C: Process Creation using fork() and exec()
examples.description: Creates a child process using fork() and replaces its memory space with a new program using exec()
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates process creation using fork() and exec(). The parent process creates a child process using fork(), and the child process replaces its memory space with the 'ls' program using execlp().

examples.title: C: Process Termination using exit()
examples.description: Terminates a process using the exit() system call
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates process termination using the exit() system call. The process prints a message and then terminates with an exit status of 0.

examples.title: C: Interprocess Communication using pipe()
examples.description: Demonstrates interprocess communication between a parent and child process using a pipe
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates interprocess communication between a parent and child process using a pipe. The parent process writes a message to the pipe, and the child process reads the message from the pipe.
